untranslatable_reason: Cup and Handle is a visual pattern recognition signal requiring multi-bar shape analysis (cup formation, handle formation, cup lip identification, height measurement). The allowed indicators do not include pattern detection functions or the ability to identify: (1) the lowest low point within a cup formation, (2) the right cup lip high, (3) the handle formation geometry, or (4) multi-bar shape validation. The signal requires custom pattern recognition logic beyond the scope of simple indicator comparisons. Pivot points were used as a placeholder for breakout logic, but this does not capture the actual Cup and Handle mechanics.
translation_notes: The Cup and Handle pattern is a chartist pattern that requires identifying specific structural elements (cup shape, handle pullback, breakout point) across multiple bars. This cannot be reliably expressed using only price levels and technical indicators. A proper implementation would require: (a) a dedicated pattern detection module, (b) local minima/maxima identification over a rolling window, or (c) shape-matching algorithms. The entry condition (close > pivot) is a generic breakout proxy only.
